description: User input for key is directly converted to int without try-except, which can raise ValueError for non-numeric input
suggested_fix: Wrap the int conversion in a try-except block and provide user-friendly error message

description: String concatenation in loop using `result += character` is inefficient for large inputs
suggested_fix: Use list to collect characters and join at the end

description: No validation for alphabet parameter - empty or invalid alphabets can cause issues
suggested_fix: Add validation to ensure alphabet is not empty and contains unique characters

description: Brute force function returns all possible decryptions, which could be a security risk for sensitive data
suggested_fix: Add parameter to limit results or return only likely decryptions

description: User input for menu choice not validated for non-numeric input before strip
suggested_fix: Add validation to ensure choice is one of the expected options

description: Missing type hints for parameters and return types in _normalize_graph method
suggested_fix: Add type hints: def _normalize_graph(self, sources: list[int], sinks: list[int]) -> None:

description: _normalize_graph returns None when sources or sinks are empty, but this leads to None source_index/sink_index which causes errors later
suggested_fix: Raise ValueError instead of returning None, or set default values with proper handling

description: The method modifies the input graph directly by inserting rows/columns, which is unexpected side effect
suggested_fix: Create a copy of the graph before modification, or document this behavior clearly

description: Class lacks docstring explaining its purpose and usage
suggested_fix: Add comprehensive docstring for FlowNetwork class

description: Missing type hints for parameters and return type in dijkstra function
suggested_fix: Add type hints: def dijkstra(graph: dict, start: str, end: str) -> int:

description: Returns -1 when no path exists, which could be confused with valid cost of -1 if negative edges were allowed
suggested_fix: Return None or raise NoPathError instead of -1

description: No validation that start and end nodes exist in the graph
suggested_fix: Add check at beginning and raise KeyError if nodes are missing

description: File lacks header docstring explaining the purpose and usage
suggested_fix: Add a module-level docstring describing the implementation

description: Unnecessary custom queue implementation (MyQueue) instead of using collections.deque
suggested_fix: Replace MyQueue with collections.deque for better performance and readability

description: MyNode class is included in the same file as AVLtree, could be separated for better organization
suggested_fix: Move MyNode to a separate file or make it an inner class of AVLtree

description: Print tree method uses inefficient custom queue with fixed-size checks (range 100) and math.pow calculations
suggested_fix: Simplify with standard BFS using collections.deque and proper level tracking

description: Delete method does not handle case where node to delete is not found
suggested_fix: Add check and raise ValueError or return False when node is not found

description: Class name inconsistency: class is BST but docstring refers to BinarySearchTree
suggested_fix: Rename class to BinarySearchTree or update docstring to match

description: Search method raises IndexError with custom message when tree is empty, which is misleading
suggested_fix: Raise a more appropriate exception like ValueError or create a custom TreeEmptyError

description: find_kth_smallest uses O(n) extra space to store all elements, should use O(h) space with inorder traversal
suggested_fix: Implement kth smallest using iterative inorder traversal with stack

description: Method names are inconsistent: del_node vs delete, and some methods have underscores vs not
suggested_fix: Standardize method names to follow PEP 8 conventions
